programme.desc: Jesse Palmer challenges the chefs to craft a large-format dessert with a decorative sugar element that embodies their assigned element, earth, air, fire, water, or space; Duff Goldman and Jacques Torres judge the striking sugar showpieces.

programme.title: Alex vs. America
programme.desc: Alex Guarnaschelli and Eric Adjepong watch three skilled chefs take on a pastry challenge; chefs from Kentucky, California and New Jersey take their shot at knocking Alex out of the top spot.

programme.title: Alex vs. America
programme.desc: Host Eric Adjepong welcomes three modernist cuisine experts from New Jersey, Colorado and South Carolina; these chefs are ready to stretch the boundaries of food as they try to outcook chef Alex Guarnaschelli.

programme.title: Alex vs. America
programme.desc: Three chocolatiers from Tennessee, Florida and California take on classically-trained chef Alex Guarnaschelli in a competition for dessert domination.

programme.title: Alex vs. America
programme.desc: Eric Adjepong invites three competitors from Alex Guarnaschelli's home city of New York to represent the city's diverse cuisine; judges Marcus Samuelsson and Leah Cohen keep the Big Apple in mind as the chefs use their skills to vie for the top spot.

programme.title: Beat Bobby Flay
programme.desc: Bobby Flay is haunted when Food Network's Amanda Freitag and Katie Lee bring two ghosts from his past to the arena; Allison Fasano, Bobby's former sous chef, and executive chef Paul Del Favero battle for the right to give Bobby a fright.

programme.title: Beat Bobby Flay
programme.sub-title: Chop of the Mornin' to Ya
programme.desc: There's expertise in spades when

programme.title: Beat Bobby Flay
programme.desc: Michael Symon and comedian Chris Redd leave Bobby Flay in tears while chefs Adam Pawlak and Page Pressley impress with their takes on a classic.

programme.title: Beat Bobby Flay
programme.desc: Hip-hop icon Lil Jon and Anne Burrell get Bobby Flay's kitchen turned up with lights, beats and bottle service; chefs Eva Pesantez and Robert Butts have Bobby on the VIP list.

programme.title: Beat Bobby Flay
programme.desc: Michael Symon enlists Clinton Kelly to distract Bobby with their friendship.

programme.title: Beat Bobby Flay
programme.desc: Bobby Flay gets shaken and stirred when Taraji P. Henson and Amanda Freitag try to mix up a defeat with pastry chefs Maurice Shelton and Cristina Vázquez.

programme.title: Beat Bobby Flay
programme.desc: Michael Symon and Madison Keys serve Bobby a curveball with a family duo.

programme.title: Beat Bobby Flay
programme.desc: Chefs Poppy O'Toole and Mika Leon lost once to Bobby Flay and they're back to ensure that doesn't happen again; Antonia Lofaso and Sophie Flay root them on from the sidelines.

programme.title: The Ultimate Baking Championship
programme.desc: Jesse Palmer asks the remaining four chefs to create arctic-themed cakes with a frozen layer; the top two chefs move on to the finale, and the bottom two chefs battle in a sudden-death round, hoping to impress judges Duff Goldman and Molly Yeh.

programme.title: Beat Bobby Flay
programme.desc: Abbott Elementary's Quinta Brunson and Chef Michael Voltaggio try to give Bobby Flay detention with their star pupils, chefs Devan Cunningham and Justin Jacobs.

programme.title: Beat Bobby Flay

programme.title: Iron Resurrection
programme.desc: It takes a lot to faze Joe Martin, but when his next job pulls into MSB it looks more like a truck load of spare parts than a 1970 C10; Joe knows exactly what he wants to do; new frame, running gear, and a super charged LSA engine.

programme.title: Iron Resurrection
programme.desc: Gary Hopper, a longtime friend of Joe, brings a '55 Chevy Bel Air to the shop; because this '55 has sentimental value to Gary's family, the pressure is on to do this build right; but missing pieces and a dented hood throw a wrench into the job.

programme.title: Iron Resurrection
programme.desc: Joe Martin is excited to see a straight '63 Impala with a four speed and legendary 409 engine under the hood; the owner wants to keep it original with the main mods under the body, new frame, suspension and big wheels.

programme.title: Iron Resurrection
programme.desc: A new customer, comes into the shop with an exceptional 1963 Cadillac; this car screams luxury but needs an upgrade; with Shane giving the MBC team creative freedom on the build, Joe gets to work on a plan; new engine, new wheels and a new interior.

programme.title: Iron Resurrection
programme.desc: Return customer Lee Farris comes to Martin Bros with a 1956 Chevy Bel Air, his first car that he wants to bring back to life; the first thing Joe notices is the outdated flame paint job.

programme.title: Iron Resurrection
programme.desc: Joe Martin loves to push the limits; when a customer rolls into the shop with a 1950 Mercury Coupe, Joe Martin knows he needs to chop the top.

programme.title: Iron Resurrection
programme.desc: With the 1950 Mercury successfully chopped, the rest of the work can begin; the MBC team now has to marry the old roof with the new geometry of the newly chopped body; there are angles everywhere, and they are all out of whack.

programme.title: Iron Resurrection
programme.desc: Joe Martin loves Novas and his eyes light up when a new customer, Eric, wants him to customize a 1972 as a tribute to his brother, if they can find one; Shag and Amanda hunt down a '71, in what appears to be mint condition.

programme.title: Iron Resurrection
programme.desc: Joe Martin and his crew are always under the gun, but their latest build, a '71 Nova, is well behind schedule and they have a ton of work to do; Joe is really going to town on the metal work on this car, inside and out.
